description: Canonical initial set of Setup-tab tasks seeded by scripts/bootstrap.mjs on first fork-clone. Format reserved for consumption by the Setup tab UI shipping in OPORD 202604-022 — downstream code may add fields but should not break existing field semantics without bumping schema_version.

items.title: Configure site title
items.body:
Edit the `SITE_TITLE` constant in `astro.config.mjs`, or set the `TABULAKIT_TITLE` environment variable at build time. The site title is the single config value exposed by the v2 scaffold per OPORD 202604-019 acceptance criteria.

**Where:** `astro.config.mjs` line ~7 (`const SITE_TITLE = ...`).

**Test:** Run `npm run dev` and confirm the title appears in the browser tab and Starlight nav bar.

items.title: Add your first content page
items.body:
Create a new `.md` or `.mdx` file in `src/content/docs/` with frontmatter:

```yaml
---
title: Page Title
description: One-sentence description for SEO/nav
---
```

Then add it to the sidebar in `astro.config.mjs`.

**Reference:** [Getting Started guide](src/content/docs/guides/getting-started.md) in this repo.

items.title: Confirm deployment target
items.body:
v2 default is GitHub Pages via `.github/workflows/deploy.yml` (shipped in OPORD 019 Phase 2).

**To enable:** Go to repo Settings → Pages → Source: GitHub Actions.

**Netlify preserved** as a secondary target (`netlify.toml`). Deploy-path simplification evaluation is scheduled under OPORD 202604-021.

items.title: Customize theme (optional)
items.body:
Starlight ships with dark/light toggle by default. To customize colors or fonts:

- Add `src/styles/custom.css` with CSS custom property overrides
- Reference it in `astro.config.mjs` via `starlight({ customCss: ['./src/styles/custom.css'] })`

**Starlight theming docs:** https://starlight.astro.build/guides/css-and-tailwind/

items.title: Configure custom domain (optional)
items.body:
If deploying to GitHub Pages:

1. Add a `CNAME` file at the repo root with your domain
2. Configure DNS (CNAME record for www → `<you>.github.io`, or A records for apex)
3. Enable HTTPS in repo Settings → Pages → Enforce HTTPS

Skip if the default `*.github.io` URL is fine.
